title: Campanula brevibracteata
taxonomy: Campanula brevibracteata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. It belongs to the genus Campanula, which comprises a large group of annuals and perennials. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in dry to moist areas. Campanula brevibracteata is further classified as a member of the Campanula sect. intro: Campanula brevibracteata, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
